title: M3 v3.2 audit patch spike (B1/B2/B3 remediation)
why: M3 v3 has three documented blindspots: B1 (no upper bound on Sharpe → 30% false-positive rate on trivial nulls per [phase5/audit_null_distribution.md](phase5/audit_null_distribution.md)), B2 (regime_diversity gate false-positives on single-sided strategies in opposite-direction regimes), B3 (3-layer CV minimum-sample compounding rejects regime-gated H4 as ValueError). All three were deferred from the spike to avoid audit-design-leak; patches must be derived from the null distribution + independently-justified percentiles, not reverse-engineered from this spike's outcomes.
value_rationale: H (unblocks H4 + regime-gating evaluation; closes audit's known false-positive surface)
scope: separate spike branch (NOT `feat/phase5-ai-hypothesizer`). **Patch architecture is NOT pre-committed.** Sequence is fixed: (1) Run B0008's expanded probe matrix to completion. (2) Write `phase5/PATCH_DESIGN_PREREGISTRATION.md` deriving candidate patch shapes WITHOUT reference to v3 outcomes — each candidate must specify (a) what null-distribution property motivates it, (b) the percentile of the null at which it would fire, (c) the false-positive rate under that null. (3) Only after the preregistration doc lands, propose specific patches. Patch design itself must pass its own DA review. v3.2 must NOT re-audit this spike's proposals. The B4 portfolio blindspot recognized in B0019a folds into this scope at step 2.

history.reason: feat(phase5): close 7 tickets â€” Loop A/H4 infra hardening + reporting
